metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the ADR's status/scope whether `region_gated_floor`, content-addressed region detection, and the typed keyspaces are (a) already implemented (cite the code locations) or (b) forward-decisions not yet implemented (mark the ADR `Proposed` and note the implementation gap explicitly, so future readers know what is live vs. planned).
findings.finding: Several mechanism details the ADR is asked to document — region_gated_floor conjunct, its UNKNOWN==raise tri-state fail-safe, content-addressed region detection, typed session/change keyspaces, and the local→Gerrit seeding prohibition — have no backing implementation in src/rebar/llm/code_review/ or the shared kernel. Writing an ADR that presents these as accepted/implemented decisions (mirroring ADR 0008's record-of-implementation stance) would misrepresent the system state to future readers and implementers.
findings.location: What section / AC line 1 (ADR content list)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Implementation status' note to the ADR draft: list each mechanism element with its status (IMPLEMENTED at <module> vs. DECIDED, not yet implemented). For any un-implemented element, set ADR status to 'Proposed' rather than 'Accepted', matching ADR 0008's pattern of documenting a fully-implemented decision.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either define 'dso-style arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop' concretely in the plan (or ADR scope) with a pointer to its origin (an external paper, a prior internal proposal, or a named alternative), or rephrase AC2 to describe the rejected approach in terms already present in the project's ADR vocabulary.
findings.finding: AC2 requires the ADR to record the explicit decision that 'NO dso-style arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop is added, and why the lightweight novelty floor suffices.' The term 'dso-style arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop' appears nowhere in the project's ADR corpus, documentation, or codebase — a reader of the ADR cannot evaluate the soundness of the rejection without knowing what is being rejected.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'The ADR records the explicit decision that NO dso-style arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop is added'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'permanent' means in the code-review novelty floor context (e.g., 'persists across patchset revisions for the same Gerrit change_id', 'until the change is abandoned/merged', etc.) so the ADR can document a concrete, verifiable divergence from plan-review's session-scoped behavior.
findings.finding: The plan describes 'permanent low-impact drop as intended compromise with the priority floor as escape hatch' as a design decision to document in the ADR, but the relationship between 'permanent' here and the existing `rising_floor_drop` behavior (which is explicitly session-scoped and resets per review per ADR 0008 §'Within-session suppression') is unspecified. The plan does not define whether 'permanent' means cross-session, cross-change, or something else, leaving the ADR author without the vocabulary to document this divergence.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: 'permanent low-impact drop as intended compromise with the priority floor as escape hatch'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief description in the Why or What section of what 'dso-style arbiter / relation-taxonomy / oscillation-loop' refers to and why it was considered and rejected, so AC 2 is verifiable from plan text alone.
findings.finding: AC 2 references 'dso-style arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop' — a cluster of concepts not introduced, named, or explained anywhere in the Why, What, or Scope sections. An implementer tasked with writing the ADR cannot derive from the plan body what this refers to, what was considered, or why it was rejected, making the criterion unverifiable from plan text alone.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Why' or 'What' section, add one sentence such as: 'Unlike the plan-review DSO mechanism (which uses a dedicated arbiter, relation-taxonomy, and oscillation-loop for convergence), code-review novelty uses only the lightweight rising-floor + region gate; the ADR must explicitly record this decision and its rationale.' This anchors the AC 2 terminology in the plan body.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the complete tri-state value vocabulary for `region_gated_floor` (e.g., `CHANGED` / `UNCHANGED` / `UNKNOWN`) and specify the semantics of each state before the ADR is written, so the decision rule is fully co-located with the schema the ADR documents.
findings.finding: The `region_gated_floor` conjunct is introduced as a named new decision construct in the plan's 'What' section, but its complete tri-state value vocabulary is never defined — only a parenthetical `(UNKNOWN==raise)` appears, leaving the other two states (and whether they are CHANGED/UNCHANGED or something else) unspecified. The ADR author cannot faithfully document a processing protocol for an enum whose value set is not stated.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: 'the new region_gated_floor conjunct and its tri-state fail-safe (UNKNOWN==raise)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Vocabulary' subsection to the plan's 'What' list: e.g., 'The `region_gated_floor` tri-state is `{CHANGED, UNCHANGED, UNKNOWN}`: UNCHANGED → the drop conjunct may fire; CHANGED → drop suppressed (the citation region was edited); UNKNOWN → raise (fail-closed, not fail-open), because an unresolvable region check must never silently permit a drop.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the exact file that is the 'code-review gate doc' to update (e.g. `docs/review-kernel.md` consumer section), or explicitly add creation of `docs/code-review-gate.md` to the What/Scope sections so the update target is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan names 'code-review gate doc' as an existing artifact to update, but no standalone code-review gate doc (e.g. docs/code-review-gate.md) exists in the repository. The code-review gate documentation is spread across sections of docs/review-kernel.md and docs/llm-framework.md. Treating a non-existent file as an update target makes the scope ambiguous: is the plan also creating this doc, or updating the review-kernel.md consumer section?
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) replace 'code-review gate doc' with 'docs/review-kernel.md (the code-review consumer section)' throughout the plan, or (b) add 'docs/code-review-gate.md — CREATE (parallel to plan-review-gate.md)' to the Scope and What sections, making it explicit that this doc is being created, not merely updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before writing the ADR, resolve and document what 'content-addressed region detection' means concretely (e.g., SHA of the diff hunk text vs. the Gerrit revision identifier) and cite any prior art or prototype demonstrating the rebase/force-push resilience property.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'content-addressed resilient region detection' is rebase/force-push resilient, but no prior ADR, existing code module, or referenced design document validates this property for a code-review citation-region hashing scheme. The plan's own 'What' section names this as a decision the ADR must capture, yet points to no prior art or existing implementation that demonstrates the resilience claim.
findings.location: What section — 'content-addressed region detection and its resilience to rebase/force-push'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'What' section specifying the region key construction (e.g., 'a SHA-256 over the normalised hunk text of the cited region, independent of line numbers and revision SHAs') and cite whether sidecar.change_fingerprint's diff_sha approach is the intended basis or a separate per-hunk hash is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC4 by specifying what 'cross-link' means (e.g. 'Markdown hyperlinks to the relevant section of docs/reuse-surface.md from the ADR and gate doc') so the criterion is unambiguously evaluable.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has a concrete proving command; all four ACs are verified only by reading the produced files, but 'cross-link' in AC4 is undefined (hyperlink? prose mention? section heading?), making that criterion not unambiguously evaluable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'docs cross-link the shared kernel primitives vs the gate-specific region gate'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC4 as: 'The ADR and gate doc each contain a Markdown hyperlink to the shared-primitives section of docs/reuse-surface.md, and reuse-surface.md contains a hyperlink back to the ADR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'typed session/change keyspaces' means structurally (e.g., a discriminated union, a string prefix convention like `session:<id>` vs `change:<change_id>`, or a Python `Literal` type) so the ADR can document a verifiable, not merely narrative, boundary.
findings.finding: The plan references `typed session/change keyspaces` as a design decision the ADR must capture, but 'typed' is never defined structurally — no schema, enum, Python type, or string-prefix convention exists in the codebase or docs that a reader can point to. Without this definition, the ADR's treatment of the keyspace separation decision will be prose-only and non-verifiable.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section: 'typed session/change keyspaces and why local must not seed a change's first Gerrit review'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'surfaced-only prior set' explicitly in the plan (e.g., 'the advisory findings from the prior sidecar, excluding blocking findings, fed to the novelty sub-call as prior context') so the ADR can document a concrete, code-traceable definition.
findings.finding: The plan refers to a 'surfaced-only prior set' as a distinct named concept the ADR covers, but this term has no definition in any existing schema, doc, or code. The code-review sidecar (`sidecar.py`) emits `blocking` and `advisory` lists but has no field named `surfaced_only` or equivalent; plan-review uses `advisory_findings` from the sidecar. Without a definition, the ADR author must invent the semantics, risking a mismatch with the eventual implementation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale to the ADR for why UNKNOWN==raise is the correct fail-safe for region_gated_floor (e.g., 'because an unknown region means we cannot apply the gate at all, and a false-pass is worse than a gate error'), explicitly contrasting it with the kernel's novelty 0.0 default and noting whether a --force escape exists.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the region_gated_floor has a tri-state fail-safe where UNKNOWN==raise, but gives no positive rationale for why this inverts the kernel's safety direction. The existing kernel novelty sub-call fail-safe is deliberately fail-safe in the opposite direction: a missing/garbage novelty answer maps to 0.0 (carryover → never dropped), because dropping a finding is the dangerous action. If region detection fails and UNKNOWN raises (i.e., fail-closed), that is a different safety posture that must be justified in the ADR — and the plan does not state why raising is correct for region detection whereas the kernel defaults to the conservative no-drop direction.
findings.location: What section — 'the new region_gated_floor conjunct and its tri-state fail-safe (UNKNOWN==raise)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact file path of the 'code-review gate doc' in the What section and in AC3 (e.g., `docs/gates/code-review.md` or `docs/code-review-gate.md`) so the canonical target for updates and cross-links is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The 'code-review gate doc' is referenced throughout the plan and ACs but its file path is never named, leaving the implementer without a canonical target to update or create.
findings.location: ## What — 'Update docs (code-review gate doc + docs/reuse-surface.md)'; AC line: 'The code-review gate doc documents the novelty convergence behavior'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to the What section: 'Update docs (docs/gates/code-review.md — or confirm canonical path — and docs/reuse-surface.md)' and mirror the resolved path in AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve 'code-review gate doc' to a concrete file path (e.g., `docs/review-kernel.md` or a new `docs/code-review-gate.md`) in the What, Scope, and all four ACs so the implementer has an unambiguous edit target.
findings.finding: The plan's 'code-review gate doc' edit target has no resolvable file path anywhere in the plan or the repo. No file named 'code-review-gate.md', 'code-review.md', or equivalent exists under docs/; the closest candidates are docs/review-kernel.md (which covers the shared kernel and the code-review consumer seam) and docs/review-policy.md. The plan's What section, Scope, and all four ACs reference this unnamed doc, leaving the implementer without a canonical edit target.
findings.location: What section ('Update docs (code-review gate doc + docs/reuse-surface.md)'); Scope; AC 3 ('The code-review gate doc documents the novelty convergence behavior'); AC 4 ('docs cross-link the shared kernel primitives vs the gate-specific region gate')
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to update the existing consumer-seam documentation, replace 'code-review gate doc' with `docs/review-kernel.md` throughout. If a new standalone gate doc is intended, add `docs/code-review-gate.md` as an explicit new file creation target in the What and Scope sections.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace '00NN' in the ADR filename with the next available ADR sequence number (e.g. inspect docs/adr/ for the highest-numbered file and increment) or state the default convention explicitly so the executor can proceed without inspection.
findings.finding: The ADR filename placeholder '00NN' is an unresolved placeholder that forces the executor to determine the next ADR sequence number by inspecting the existing docs/adr/ directory — a real but minor defaultable gap.
findings.suggested_fix: State 'choose the next available number by inspecting docs/adr/; as of this writing the next number is 00NN — confirm before writing' or simply resolve the number at plan-authoring time.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the code-review gate doc by its exact file path (e.g. docs/gates/code-review.md) in both the Scope and AC3 so the executor can locate it without a repo search.
findings.finding: The 'code-review gate doc' referenced in the Scope and ACs is never named by file path, forcing the executor to discover it by searching the repo.
findings.location: Scope section ('code-review gate doc'); AC3 ('The code-review gate doc documents...')
findings.suggested_fix: Add the explicit path, e.g. 'docs/gates/code-review.md (or whichever file currently documents the code-review gate)' in the Scope line and AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `00NN` placeholder with the resolved ADR number (currently `0039`, verify against latest merged ADRs at implementation time) in the What section and anywhere the filename appears.
findings.finding: The ADR filename `docs/adr/00NN-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` uses an unresolved numeric placeholder. The existing ADR sequence ends at `0038-governance-artifacts.md`, making `0039` the next available slot — but the plan leaves resolution to the implementer with no guidance. Previous review cache already flagged this as requiring manual inspection of the directory.
findings.suggested_fix: Change `00NN` to `0039` and add a note: '(verify no concurrent ADR was merged; increment if so).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the Scope as an edit target (or explicitly note it is out of scope) so readers can find the new ADR via the doc index.
findings.finding: docs/README.md catalogs every doc file in the repo and would need a new entry for the ADR created by this plan, but it is not listed in the Scope or enumerated as an edit target.
findings.location: Scope section: 'docs/adr/, code-review gate doc, docs/reuse-surface.md'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC with a concrete validation mechanism, e.g. a checklist that maps each design point named in 'Why' (tri-state fail-safe, typed keyspaces, content-addressed region detection, etc.) to a specific ADR section, so a reviewer can confirm coverage rather than just existence.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism is specified: all four ACs check artifact existence or content ('ADR captures…', 'ADR records…', 'gate doc documents…', 'docs cross-link…') but none describes a before/after workflow comparison, an operational metric, or a dogfooding step that confirms the documentation actually prevents future re-litigation or accidental forking of the novelty definition.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC5: 'Each design decision listed in the Why section (rising_floor_drop reuse, region_gated_floor tri-state, surfaced-only prior set, permanent low-impact drop, typed session/change keyspaces, content-addressed region detection, file-level v1 deferral) maps to a named section or decision record in the ADR — verified by cross-referencing the Why list against the ADR table of contents during review.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence in the ADR's 'Context' or plan's 'Why' section stating WHY a new ADR (rather than an extension of review-kernel.md) is the right artifact — e.g., because this documents deliberate design divergences (region gate vs. whole-artifact floor, no dso-arbiter) that need decision-record status, not just reference documentation.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' and 'What' sections give no positive rationale for the chosen documentation approach (separate ADR + code-review gate doc + reuse-surface.md) rather than extending the existing review-kernel.md, which already has a 'Code-review consumer (b744)' section that documents the gate's plug-points. ADR 0008's rationale is grounded in 'this mechanism is distinct from prior ADRs on adjacent axes'; no equivalent framing is given here.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
